question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 10?

fragility_points: Single-minute settlement rather than average or end-of-day price.
key_assumptions: BTC/USDT will not suffer a large enough downside move before noon ET on April 10 to push the Binance 1-minute close below 70,000.
Binance remains the operative venue without a settlement-relevant anomaly or dislocation.
main_logical_chain: The rules say settlement is the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET April 10 one-minute candle close, strictly above 70,000.
Current Binance spot and nearby 1-minute closes are in the low 72.3k range, materially above the threshold.
Outside-view persistence therefore favors Yes, but exact-minute and venue-specific settlement risk prevents treating No as negligible.
That supports a 90% estimate: strong favorite, but slightly below the live mid-90s market pricing.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is already trading comfortably above 70,000 on Binance with less than a day left, so Yes is the base-rate favorite, though a single exact-minute settlement keeps the probability below the most extreme market pricing.
quote_anchors: Binance BTCUSDT last price fetched during the run was 72,363.48.
The final close must be strictly greater than 70,000; equal to 70,000 would be No.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality primary evidence on rules and exchange price; medium evidence independence because the secondary cross-check likely reflects overlapping exchange data; source-of-truth ambiguity is low after reading the contract rules.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact future 1-minute close, so a normal crypto downside swing or Binance-specific wick could still produce No.
A roughly 3.4% cushion is meaningful but not huge for BTC over a sub-24-hour window.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was 72,363.48 during the run, about 3.4% above the threshold.
Recent Binance 1-minute closes were also in the low 72.3k range, close to the actual settlement object.
CoinGecko roughly confirmed the same price regime, reducing concern that Binance was showing an outlier print.
payload.timing_relevance: Timing is central: the market settles on the Binance 12:00 ET April 10 1-minute close, and this run verified Binance timestamps against ET to confirm the analysis was done about 19 hours before resolution.
unresolved_ambiguities: How often BTC makes 3%+ downside moves from similar starting conditions over the next ~19 hours was not quantified directly in this run.
Live Polymarket price observed on page fetch differed from assignment baseline, suggesting some intraday baseline drift.
payload.what_would_change_view: A fresh Binance check showing BTC much closer to 70,000, evidence that 3%+ sub-24-hour downside moves are more common than assumed, or signs of Binance-specific instability would lower the estimate.
